key_evidence: HRW January 2026: Saudi Arabia executed 356 people in 2025, setting second consecutive annual record (345 in 2024). 243 drug-related; 198 nonviolent drug offenses; majority foreign nationals.
70-year-old academic died in Saudi prison April 2026; torture-tainted confessions systemic.
NEOM Huwaitat tribe forcible evictions continuing; NGO intervention at UN HRC61 March 17, 2026.
Vision 2030 reforms real (women workforce 19.7% -> 36.3%) but coexist with detention of the women's rights activists who championed those reforms.

Published BND score of 2.0 (raw) overstates boundary integrity given migrant labor, NEOM displacement, and detention patterns; research assigns BND 2.13 (14.1 scaled) reflecting partial consent frameworks alongside systemic failures.
flag_rationale: Delta +5.0 is exactly at threshold. Net upward movement is a floor-clamping/methodology correction (several published subdims are 1.0=0.0 scaled, research finds non-zero baseline compassion infrastructure). This is NOT a substantive improvement signal — execution record is worsening. Recommend human review on whether to apply floor adjustment OR hold at published 4.4 pending methodology review of floor-clamping display artifacts (same family as Haiti/South Sudan issue flagged Apr 19).
methodology_flag: Floor-clamping — published composite 4.4 reflects aggressive 1.0-raw assignments in 3 subdims; research finds non-zero practice baselines. Same methodology family as Apr 19 Haiti/South Sudan methodology_notes.
